title: UNISA Registration Fee 2026 South Africa | Fees & Payment Guide
metaTitle: UNISA Registration Fee 2026 South Africa | Complete Guide
metaDescription: UNISA registration fee 2026: no application fee, but tuition from R4,200/year. See the full 2026 fee structure, NSFAS info, and payment deadlines.
content:
UNISA Registration Fee 2026 South Africa

The University of South Africa (UNISA) is the largest open-distance-learning university in Africa, serving over 400,000 students. Many students ask about UNISA's registration and tuition fees for 2026. This guide covers everything you need to know about UNISA's fee structure, payment deadlines, and financial aid options.

UNISA Application Fee 2026

Good news: UNISA has NO application fee for undergraduate applicants. Applying to UNISA is completely free. You only start paying once you have been accepted and you register for your modules.

UNISA Registration Fees 2026

UNISA's fees are calculated per module (they use a credit system). Here is an overview of what you can expect to pay in 2026:

Fee Type2026 Amount (approx.)Notes
Application feeR0 (FREE)No application fee at UNISA
Registration fee (initial)R135–R300Administrative registration fee
Tuition per module (3-credit)R1,800–R3,500Varies by faculty and level
Tuition per module (6-credit)R3,200–R5,500Higher-credit modules cost more
Annual study material & library feeIncluded in module feeStudy guides posted or available online
Technology levyR135–R270myUnisa digital platform access
Examination fee per moduleIncluded in module feeFinal exams included in tuition

Typical Annual UNISA Tuition Costs by Qualification (2026)

QualificationTypical Annual CostDuration
BCom (3-year degree)R12,000–R22,000/year3 years minimum
BEd (Education)R14,000–R20,000/year4 years minimum
LLB (Law)R14,000–R22,000/year4 years minimum
BSocSci / BAR10,000–R18,000/year3 years minimum
BScR14,000–R22,000/year3 years minimum
Advanced Certificates / Short coursesR3,000–R8,000/year6 months to 1 year

Note: UNISA fees are updated annually. The amounts above are approximate 2026 figures. Always verify the current fees on the UNISA website when registering.

UNISA Payment Deadlines 2026

UNISA's payment structure for 2026:

  • Semester 1 (January intake): Registration October–November 2025, initial payment due by January 2026
  • Semester 2 (July intake): Registration April–May 2026, payment due by June/July 2026
  • 50% upfront payment: UNISA requires you to pay at least 50% of your fees upfront or arrange a payment plan
  • Full payment deadline: All fees must be paid before exam results are released

NSFAS Funding at UNISA 2026

UNISA students can qualify for NSFAS (National Student Financial Aid Scheme) funding. NSFAS at UNISA covers:

  • Tuition fees
  • Learning material allowance (for study guides, textbooks, etc.)
  • Living allowance (for students who meet specific criteria)

To qualify for NSFAS at UNISA, your household income must be below R350,000 per year. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za. Applications for 2026 funding typically open in October 2025.

UNISA Fee Exemptions and Discounts

  • Fee remission: UNISA offers fee remissions for registered UNISA staff and their dependants
  • Early payment discount: Some years UNISA offers a small discount for full early payment — check the UNISA website
  • Bursaries: Many UNISA students qualify for employer bursaries, Department of Education bursaries, and private sector bursaries

How to Pay UNISA Fees 2026

UNISA accepts the following payment methods:

  • EFT (Electronic Funds Transfer): Pay directly into UNISA's bank account using your student number as reference
  • myUnisa online payment portal: Pay by card or EFT via the myUnisa portal
  • Bank payment: At any branch of the major banks using UNISA's banking details
  • Capitec Pay: UNISA is listed as a merchant on the Capitec app

How to Register at UNISA 2026

  1. Apply online at www.unisa.ac.za/apply (no application fee)
  2. Receive your student number via email after successful application
  3. Select your modules for the semester via myUnisa
  4. Pay the required fees or apply for NSFAS
  5. Receive your study material and access the myUnisa online learning platform

While studying at UNISA, use StudyBuddy's AI tutor to get personalised help with your UNISA modules — available 24/7, covering a wide range of subjects. Start free today.
quickAnswer: UNISA has NO application fee — applying is completely free. Registration/tuition fees in 2026 depend on your modules: approximately R1,800–R5,500 per module, or R10,000–R22,000 per year for most degrees. NSFAS covers fees for qualifying students (household income below R350,000/year).

faqs.question: Does UNISA charge an application fee?
faqs.answer: No. UNISA does NOT charge an application fee. Applying to UNISA is completely free. You only pay fees after you are accepted and register for your modules. This makes UNISA the most accessible university in South Africa for first-time applicants.

faqs.question: How much does UNISA cost per year in 2026?
faqs.answer: UNISA fees depend on your programme and the number of modules you take. Most undergraduate degrees cost R10,000–R22,000 per year in tuition fees. A BCom typically costs R12,000–R22,000/year, while a BEd or LLB costs R14,000–R22,000/year.

faqs.question: Can NSFAS pay for UNISA?
faqs.answer: Yes. NSFAS covers UNISA tuition and learning material fees for qualifying students. Your household income must be below R350,000 per year. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year for the following academic year.

faqs.question: When is the UNISA registration deadline for 2026?
faqs.answer: UNISA accepts students for two semesters: Semester 1 (January) with registration in October–November; and Semester 2 (July) with registration in April–May. Exact dates are published on the UNISA website. Visit www.unisa.ac.za for current registration dates.
